scope.summary: Memory KV-cache schema overlay for Agent-Attention Subtask 04.
scope.purpose: Define auditable memory read/write records, typed memory profiles, and metric events while remaining compatible with Subtask 02 memoryEntry, trajectoryEvent, failureSignal, and routeDecision.
out_of_scope: Changing the core Subtask 02 schema definitions
Storing full raw transcripts as memory values
Implementing a runtime memory store outside docs/deliverables/04

claims.claim: Reflexion and Voyager motivate storing behavioral feedback, failure reflections, and reusable skills, while Memory Networks motivate explicit read/write memory components.
The Phase 0-1 formal model already exposes memoryEntry, trajectoryEvent, routeDecision, and failureSignal fields that can support auditable memory operations.
A memory cache improves agent routing only when each read/write is provenance-bound, usefulness-labeled, and allowed to decay or enter quarantine after harm.

minimal_version: Use one canonical Subtask 02 memoryEntry object for all memory types.
Require every memory write to include memory_type, key, value summary fields, provenance.evidence_refs, write_reason, and reuse_outcomes.
Log every memory read and write as a trajectoryEvent with action_type memory_read or memory_write, memory ids, usefulness label, route scores, and evidence refs.
Compute memory_bonus with a hard cap and negative-transfer penalty before route selection.
Initialize read usefulness as unknown unless immediate verifier evidence exists; later relabel as useful, harmful, neutral, or unknown from outcome logs.
enhanced_version: Add audit envelopes around 02 memoryEntry objects to track route_signature, read/write operation ids, usefulness label provenance, freshness, quarantine status, and decay state.
Partition retrieval by task_family, memory_type, tool_schema_refs, and benchmark split before lexical or embedding scoring.
Use failure_memory as an operational profile encoded as memory_type behavior_kv with write_reason failure or negative_transfer, because 02 does not currently include a fifth memory_type enum.
Apply split-aware isolation so benchmark test memories cannot retrieve training answers unless the experiment explicitly permits cross-task transfer.
Require verifier-approved promotion before a quarantined memory can contribute positive memory_bonus again.
counterexamples: A high-similarity episodic memory from a previous repo can activate the wrong build tool and reduce success.
A stale knowledge memory can override newer tool output if memory_bonus is uncapped.
A full trajectory stored as value can dominate retrieval with irrelevant tokens and hide the causal step that mattered.
A success-only cache can repeat workflows that succeeded for accidental reasons while failing to block known avoid patterns.

alignment_with_02.memoryEntry: All persisted memories must validate against docs/deliverables/02/schemas.json#/$defs/memoryEntry before optional audit wrapping.
alignment_with_02.trajectoryEvent: Every read/write must emit a docs/deliverables/02 trajectoryEvent with action_type memory_read or memory_write.
alignment_with_02.failureSignal: Harmful reads and stale-memory regressions must create or reference failureSignal.failure_type negative_transfer, contradiction, verifier_failed, test_failed, or low_confidence.
alignment_with_02.routeDecision: Memory retrieval contributes only through routeDecision.candidates[].score_terms.memory_bonus and never bypasses candidate scoring.

knowledge_memory.contents: Accepted facts, source summaries, API constraints, benchmark facts, or repository facts.
knowledge_memory.promotion_rule: May be reused only when evidence_refs point to tool output, verifier result, or cited source summaries.

episodic_memory.contents: Compressed task trajectory, decision points, verifier outcomes, and final result.
episodic_memory.promotion_rule: Contributes to route selection only after a useful or neutral reuse outcome, or after direct verifier approval.

skill_memory.contents: Reusable procedure, checklist, command pattern, or code-edit workflow referenced by reusable_skill_ref.
skill_memory.promotion_rule: Requires successful replay or verifier-approved dry run before high confidence.

behavior_kv.contents: Route-conditioned action preference, avoid pattern, verifier choice, or failure memory encoded as behavioral key-value guidance.
behavior_kv.promotion_rule: Positive bonuses require useful_count > harmful_count; avoid-only entries may remain active while quarantined from positive bonuses.

failure_memory.status: Alias profile, not a new 02 enum value.
failure_memory.encoding: Persist as memory_type behavior_kv with write_reason failure or negative_transfer and failure_success_features containing failure signatures.
required_value_fields: successful_workflow as empty or mitigation workflow

audit_requirements.value_summary_rule: Summarize the causal reusable content in at most 600 characters and store hashes or refs for longer artifacts.

memory_bonus_guardrail.formula: memory_bonus_i = min(0.20, max(-0.40, mean_top_n(retrieval_score * usefulness_prior * route_match * freshness_decay) - negative_transfer_penalty))
memory_bonus_guardrail.fresh_evidence_override: If current task evidence contradicts memory, set positive memory contribution to 0 and emit contradiction or negative_transfer signal.
memory_bonus_guardrail.stale_memory_decay: freshness_decay = exp(-age_days / half_life_days), with default half_life_days 90 for knowledge_memory, 60 for episodic_memory, 120 for skill_memory, and 45 for behavior_kv.
memory_bonus_guardrail.quarantine_rule: If harmful_count >= 2 or negative_transfer_count >= 2, quarantine the memory from positive memory_bonus until verifier-approved promotion.

experiments.description: Create valid and invalid read/write fixtures for each memory profile and validate that every operation can be joined to 02 memoryEntry, trajectoryEvent, routeDecision, and failureSignal records.
controls: failure memory encoded as unsupported enum

experiments.description: Replay routing decisions with useful, stale, and harmful memories while holding semantic_match fixed.

risks: Encoding failure_memory as behavior_kv is compatible with 02 but may be semantically surprising.
Usefulness labels can lag behind the read event, so dashboards must support later label updates.
A strict audit envelope can increase logging volume and latency.
Freshness decay can suppress still-valid stable facts unless source evidence is reverified.
open_questions: Should Subtask 02 add failure_memory as a fifth memory_type enum, or keep it as a behavior_kv profile?
What exact memory_bonus cap should Phase 0 use: 0.10, 0.20, or a budget-normalized value?
Who is authoritative for relabeling usefulness when verifier outcome and no-memory counterfactual disagree?
Should stale knowledge memories be refreshed automatically or only marked as low confidence?
title: Agent-Attention Memory KV-Cache Audit Overlay
